title: Simplify header user navigation, remove top section breadcrumbs, and restyle History action button
description: Address feedback from avaliacao-interface.md (items 4, 5, and 6): (1) In AppHeader.tsx, the Settings gear button and User Menu chip open identical menus — remove the redundant Settings gear button and retain only the User Menu chip (dropdown-usuario.png). (2) Remove the top <Breadcrumbs /> component positioned above the section title in SectionHeader.tsx / PageContentPage.tsx so the section title starts directly with the section category eyebrow. (3) Restyle the 'Histórico' action button in SectionHeader.tsx to remove the text underline (hover:underline / link look) and present it as a clean ghost action button matching the editorial header controls.
acceptanceCriteria: The standalone Settings gear button is removed from AppHeader.tsx; user menu options (History, Home, Users, Tokens, Logout) remain accessible via the top-right User Menu chip
The <Breadcrumbs /> component above the section category title in SectionHeader.tsx / PageContentPage.tsx is removed
The 'Histórico' button in SectionHeader.tsx no longer renders with an underline; it uses a clean secondary/ghost button style matching action controls
Responsive layout (mobile drawer and tablet/desktop views) remains functional and uninhibited

steps.description: Remove redundant Settings gear icon button
steps.details: In AppHeader.tsx, remove the standalone Settings gear button so only the UserMenu chip triggers user settings and navigation options.

steps.description: Remove top breadcrumb component from section header
steps.details: In SectionHeader.tsx / PageContentPage.tsx, remove the <Breadcrumbs /> rendering above the section category title.
In SectionHeader.tsx, remove hover:underline / underline styles from the Histórico button and style it as a secondary ghost button.
Rebuild and inspect the admin shell to confirm header simplification, breadcrumb removal, and clean Histórico button styling.

technicalNotes: Removing Breadcrumbs cleans up vertical margin in SectionHeader.tsx, aligning with Zeroheight's clean document title header.
